aids.title: Islands in an Ocean
aids.content: Think of connected components as islands. Each island is a group of land masses connected by bridges (edges). If you can walk from one village to another, they are on the same island (same component). Isolated villages with no bridges are their own islands.

aids.title: Component Count = Number of DFS Calls
aids.content: The number of connected components equals the number of times you start a new DFS/BFS from the outer loop. Each fresh DFS call from an unvisited vertex discovers a new component. Count those calls to count components.
aids.forRemembering: How to count connected components using DFS

aids.title: V - C = Minimum Edges
aids.content: Remember 'V minus C' for minimum edges. V = vertices, C = components. A tree within each component uses exactly (vertices_in_component - 1) edges. Sum across all C components: total = V - C.
aids.forRemembering: Minimum number of edges given vertices and components
